author: edgl_dev
content:
I pushed an update to improve blob inclusion on ethrex, but it seems this caused grandine to lose all peers, maybe it could be a bug in grandine?

It held a stable 14 consensus peers for the whole half-hour before, then went 14 ->  0 the minute the EL restarted, and hasn't recovered in ~22 min

content: and so it isnt driving the engine api anymore

author: potuz
content:
<@412614104222531604> <@954738828445372416> first: the panda tool is freaking awesome. Second, the fork at slot 6246 (where you said Barnabas that there was a reset up to 6250)  is most likely a goose chase. The problem is that ethrex was restarted during 6248 right after lodestar-nethermind-1  6247 block. Then ethrex actually manages to propose a block in 6248 but based on 6246 and the same ethrex gets to propose twice in a row (plus lighthouse-ethrex-1 which is probably on the same situation), creating a chain of blocks that should be reorged (and they were). 

Now, there may be still a bug in Erigon as it should have never been sent an FCU to 6250 cause that chain should never have been canonical on any non-ethrex node

author: tersec
content: well, in theory this should be a measurable thing. At least Nimbus has specific logs (at debug level only) of when it receives all of these kinds of gossip messages. I would imagine some other CLs do too

author: tersec
content: so one should be able to find specific evidence of the propagation of envelopes through the network

author: tersec
content: or, e.g., Nimbus has separate logging (also at debug level) of, did it receive a message on gossip topic `$FOO` and then, did it accept or ignore or reject that message, et cetera. So even if it doesn't propagate a message, it should be visible in the logs. I would suspect that this isn't entirely unique to Nimbus
